theme: power grid survives any single line trip
title: Firebreak Grid: Hardening a Power Network Against Any Single Trip
seed_problem: capacity-budgeted cascade robustness under a worst-of-sweep single-line-trip sweep
mutation: composite: capacity-budgeting + fixed proportional load-redistribution cascade + worst-of-sweep (min over scenarios) served-demand scoring; capacities are routing, not margins
strategies: uniform baseline: split the budget equally across all lines (the checker's reference; high-base lines trip at rest and cascade)

proportional-to-load greedy: buy capacity in proportion to base load -- covers every base load but leaves only a thin uniform surge cushion, so backbone entrances trip in the worst cascade and the pocket bait wastes budget
value-at-risk hardening: cover base loads then add surge margin to the highest-cascade-value lines sized to the worst sweep surge

firebreak (strong): deliberately under-provision low-value ground-shedding pockets so cascades die there, redirecting the freed budget into surge margin on backbone entrances so every scenario's cascade stops before crossing a backbone
